document.title: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, Report No. A-TCF-5-056 Rev.1 dated APR/02/2021.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents the usability engineering process, application specification, usability-related hazards, user interface requirements, verification results, and references to usability validation plan/report for the ADD detector.
keyPoints: Manufacturer is identified as H&abyz and model name as ADD.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding mammography.
References include EN/IEC usability standards and EN ISO 14971 risk management.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are recorded as Pass with no issues.
Usability validation is referenced to Usability Validation Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040.

evidence.claim: The document is a Usability Engineering Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-056 Rev.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-056 (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: The document revision date is APR/02/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date APR/02/2021

evidence.claim: Revision 1 revised the product name.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 APR/02/2021 Revise the product name

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name: ADD
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report references EN 60601-1-6:2010 and IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6: General requirements for safety – Collateral standard: Usability IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2013

evidence.claim: The report references EN 62366:2008 and IEC 62366:2007+AM1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366 [2008] Application of usability engineering to medical devices IEC 62366:2007+AM1[2015] Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for digital imaging for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes adults and children, except pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Adult and Children ... Patient group/state: except pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended user experience is radiologist.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: The device is intended for professional hospital use.
evidence.evidenceText: Hospital, intended for professional use

evidence.claim: Operating conditions are 5℃ to 40℃, 30 to 75% relative humidity, and 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation condition - Temperature: 5℃ to 40℃ - Humidity: 30 to 75% relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: Storage conditions are -10℃ to 50℃, 10 to 80% relative humidity, and 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition - Temperature: -10℃ to 50℃ - Humidity: 10 to 80% relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 500 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include connecting power, data, LAN, trigger, and communication cables, powering on the detector, positioning the patient, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the power supply ... Power on the detector ... Arrange the patient in the correct position ... Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: The device is used in conjunction with X-ray device generator, PACS, and MWL.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray device (Generator) Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Modality Worklist (MWL)

evidence.claim: The report identifies electromagnetic interference susceptibility or emission as a safety-related characteristic.
evidence.evidenceText: Device could potentially emit, or be affected by, electromagnetic interference, refer to EMC test report.

evidence.claim: The ADD requires periodic calibration.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD will need periodic calibration.

evidence.claim: The report identifies the device as portable.
evidence.evidenceText: Is the Medical device intended to be mobile or portable? A Portable device

evidence.claim: Use scenarios for worst case include wiring disconnection, not attending to detector readiness, wrong interface wiring, dropping, excessive force, wrong calibration, high temperature, water/humidity, patient positioning by holding the detector, mode selection, and network configuration.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Errors - Wrong wiring of interface cable from X-ray machine to Detector - Dropping - Applying excessive force - Wrong calibration in the control software

evidence.claim: UI requirements include user-friendly instructions, instructions for power-on, operation checks, cleaning, storage, simple use method, inability to control output, and indicators.
evidence.evidenceText: Write user-friendly, easy-to-understand instructions ... Add the instructions how to turn on the device ... Can’t control output ... Add the indicator

evidence.claim: The implemented UI checklist includes power button, accessories, LED indicator, manual understanding, safety instruction, language, and product label.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 Button Power Button ... UI-07 Product Label Understanding Symbols and description

evidence.claim: Usability verification result for UI-01 through UI-07 is Pass with no issues.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 No issues Pass ... UI-07 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: Usability validation is documented by reference to Usability Validation Report A-TCF-5-040.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040)

evidence.claim: The conclusion states residual risk can be accepted after overall residual risk assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Through overall residual risk assessment, we are confirmed that overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; source metadata suggests MFDS/domestic while source path suggests CE MDD/Dimdi context.
Product signal in metadata includes HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the source text identifies the model only as ADD.
Several OCR/text quality issues are present, including phrases such as 'Person suing' and 'Men by which'.

Some checklist rows contain internally inconsistent wording, such as N/A entries that state 'There is risk' and sterilization wording that conflicts with earlier 'Not any parts are intended to be sterilized'.
The document references usability validation plan/report documents but does not include their contents.
